skill.name: scientific-critical-thinking
skill.description: Evaluate scientific claims, research methods, bias, statistics, and evidence quality using structured critical appraisal frameworks.
skill.summary: Apply structured scientific critique to studies, claims, methods, statistics, and evidence quality.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk cryptography and reconnaissance matches, but reviewed content shows educational markdown prose, not executable malicious logic. The only confirmed risk factor is external command capability because SKILL.md declares Bash and includes command examples; no credential access, network exfiltration, prompt injection, or destructive behavior was found.

low_findings.title: External Command Capability Requires User Awareness
low_findings.description: SKILL.md declares Bash support and includes command examples for schematic generation and reference searching. The examples are not evidence of malicious behavior, but they make external command execution a real capability that users should understand before running generated commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash permission and command examples are directly present. Confidence is moderate because the commands are documented examples, not dynamic command execution or exfiltration logic.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Matches Are Markdown Prose False Positives
low_findings.description: The numerous weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance matches occur in educational reference markdown about research methods, bias, evidence quality, fallacies, and statistics. No executable cryptographic code, reconnaissance workflow, data collection, or outbound transfer behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static signals are weak because the matched files are markdown reference text. I found no semantic evidence that these matches represent executable security threats.

content.user_title: Evaluate Scientific Claims with Critical Rigor
content.value_statement: Scientific claims can be hard to assess when methods, statistics, and bias risks are hidden in dense papers. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured way to critique research quality and evidence strength.
actual_capabilities: Reviews study design, controls, blinding, sampling, and measurement quality.
Identifies cognitive, selection, measurement, analysis, and confounding biases.
Evaluates statistical reporting, p-values, effect sizes, confidence intervals, and missing data handling.
Applies evidence hierarchy, GRADE concepts, and Cochrane risk of bias principles.
Detects logical fallacies and unsupported causal claims in scientific arguments.
Suggests improvements for research questions, protocols, and reporting plans.
limitations: It does not replace expert peer review or domain specialist judgment.
It depends on the quality and completeness of the study text provided by the user.
It cannot verify unpublished data, hidden analysis decisions, or undisclosed conflicts of interest.
It may need field-specific standards supplied by the user for specialized domains.

use_cases.title: Review a Published Study
use_cases.description: Assess whether a paper's methods, statistics, and conclusions support its main claims.

use_cases.title: Check a Research Proposal
use_cases.description: Find design weaknesses, bias risks, and missing analysis details before a study begins.

use_cases.title: Evaluate a Public Science Claim
use_cases.description: Compare a media or policy claim against the strength and limits of the cited evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Claim Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one scientific claim.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this scientific claim. Identify the claim type, the evidence provided, major limitations, and what conclusion is justified.

prompt_templates.title: Study Methods Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate critique of a paper or protocol.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this study's methodology. Assess design fit, sampling, controls, blinding, measurement quality, confounding, and threats to validity.

prompt_templates.title: Statistics and Bias Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this study for statistical pitfalls and bias. Check power, test choice, assumptions, multiple comparisons, effect sizes, missing data, and selective reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Evidence Quality Synthesis
prompt_templates.prompt: Synthesize the evidence across these studies. Rate evidence strength, consistency, directness, precision, publication bias risk, and confidence in each conclusion.

output_examples.input: A paper claims that a supplement improves memory based on a small observational survey.
output_examples.output: The strongest concern is causal overreach. An observational survey can show association, but it cannot rule out confounding, selection bias, or reverse causation.

output_examples.input: A randomized trial reports p = .04 but does not report effect sizes or multiple comparison correction.
output_examples.output: The result needs cautious interpretation. Ask for the planned primary outcome, number of tested outcomes, effect size, confidence interval, and correction method.

output_examples.input: A proposal plans an intervention study without blinding or attrition handling.
output_examples.output: Key design improvements include assessor blinding, predefined attrition rules, a missing data plan, and clear separation of confirmatory and exploratory analyses.

best_practices: Provide the methods, results, limitations, and claims together when possible.
Ask for severity-ranked findings with evidence and practical recommendations.
State the domain, population, intervention, comparator, and outcome before review.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to prove a preferred conclusion from weak evidence.
Do not treat statistical significance as practical importance without effect sizes.
Do not use it as a substitute for formal ethics, clinical, or regulatory review.

faq.question: Can this skill review a full research paper?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the relevant sections, and ask for methodology, statistics, bias, and conclusion support.

faq.question: Does it use GRADE and Cochrane risk of bias?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for evidence quality and risk of bias assessment.

faq.question: Can it design a new experiment?
faq.answer: It can suggest study design improvements, controls, measurement plans, and analysis planning steps.

faq.question: Can it replace a domain expert?
faq.answer: No. It supports structured critique, but expert review is still needed for specialized claims.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Methods, sample details, outcomes, statistical tests, results, limitations, and the exact claim work best.

faq.question: Can it evaluate media summaries of science?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare public claims with the actual evidence and identify overstatement.
